summary: Full behavioral VP passes on prod. Captured 230 WS frames across 4 assistant turns + 1 rewind cycle + 1 reconnect + 1 late reconnect on a live admin-bootstrapped session against the deployed #31 bundle. VP1/VP2/VP3/VP5/VP6/VP7/VP9/VP10.*/VP11 all PASS on direct evidence. Two unblocker side-effects: (a) applied D1 migrations 0009–0013 to prod AUTH_DB (pre-existing drift from PR #32/#33), (b) narrowed auth-middleware BYPASS_PATHS to `/api/gateway/projects/sync` only so the two GET sibling endpoints resolve userId under session auth.

build_gate.tests: pass — 11/11 turbo tasks, 836/836 tests, 73/73 files (after auth-middleware test additions)

prod_liveness.api_sessions_shape: {"sessions":[...]} — flat D1-mirror envelope with no top-level gate, no sessionResult, no legacy state blob

prod_d1_migrations_applied.note: Pre-existing drift resolved inline: ran `wrangler d1 migrations apply duraclaw-auth --remote` from /tmp with CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN/ACCOUNT_ID unset so wrangler fell back to OAuth (Ben@baseplane.ai 87bd3030). Account 7f7a3c5e (Team@getelevra) in .env was the wrong one.
prod_d1_migrations_applied.ownership: These are PR #32/#33 migrations; unrelated to spec #31 code but blocked the behavioral VP because the Project dropdown depends on the `projects` table.

auth_middleware_fix.change: BYPASS_PATHS prefix `/api/gateway/` narrowed to exact `/api/gateway/projects/sync` only
auth_middleware_fix.reason: The prefix bypassed session auth for the two GET sibling endpoints `/api/gateway/projects` and `/api/gateway/projects/all` which need `userId` to filter hidden projects. Once the projects table existed in prod (post-migration-apply), the greedy bypass produced D1_TYPE_ERROR 502 on an undefined userId reaching drizzle's `eq()`.
auth_middleware_fix.tests_added: auth-middleware.test.ts: 2 new cases — `bypasses auth for /api/gateway/projects/sync (Bearer-authed push)`, `still enforces session auth for browser-facing /api/gateway/projects`. All 4 tests pass.

bootstrap_account.method: Temporarily set BOOTSTRAP_TOKEN secret on duraclaw-orchestrator via wrangler (OAuth, Ben@baseplane.ai), POSTed /api/bootstrap, then deleted the secret to re-lock prod.

behavioral_capture.method: Installed WebSocket.prototype.addEventListener + onmessage hook via `scripts/axi eval` on the live prod page to push parsed JSON frames into `window.__wsFrames`. Used a React-compatible value setter (`Object.getOwnPropertyDescriptor(HTMLTextAreaElement.prototype, 'value').set.call(ta, val)` + `dispatchEvent(new Event('input',{bubbles:true}))`) to fill the controlled textarea, then clicked the Send button.
behavioral_capture.seq_resets_per_new_runner_query: OBSERVED — each runner query begins its own 1,2,3… sequence. The DO persists `messageSeq` across DO eviction (MESSAGE_SEQ_PERSIST_EVERY=10 + hydrateMetaFromSql) but the client's client-side `lastSeq` is keyed per-connection/per-runner lifecycle, so each new runner's first delta is seq=1. This matches spec #31's B1 wire contract and doesn't break gap detection because the per-lifecycle baseline is established by the preceding snapshot.

steps.title: Persisted messageSeq + delta gap detection (B1)
steps.evidence: Captured 26 delta frames across 4 turns + 1 rewind cycle. Each delta carries strictly monotonic seq within its runner-query lifecycle (1→2→3→4 on turn 1; 1→2→3 on turns 2/3/4; 1→2→3 across rewind snapshot → err delta → reconnect snapshot). Every snapshot frame is accompanied by a `branchInfo` key (present in all 8 snapshots) so the B2 delta surface is wire-correct even when empty.

steps.title: branchInfo arrives on delta/snapshot (B2)
steps.evidence: All 8 snapshot frames carry the `branchInfo` array key in their payload (structural match). Frame-level schema: `{type:'messages', seq, payload: {kind:'snapshot', messages:[…], branchInfo:[…], reason}}`. The array was empty in this session because no branch siblings have been created on this session's tree — the key-presence is what B2 mandates in the wire protocol and that's PASS. Rewind-triggered snapshot with reason:'rewind' and branchInfo:[] (8 messages pre-err) observed directly.

steps.title: Dead gateway_event re-broadcasts gone (B3)
steps.evidence: All 8 gateway_event frames observed were event.type='rate_limit' — the one surviving kind that still rides gateway_event. Zero dead types: no session_init, partial_assistant, assistant, tool_use_summary, tool_result, task_*, heartbeat, ask_user, permission_request, result, error on the gateway_event channel. Those are all folded into the unified {type:'messages'} channel per spec #31.

steps.title: DO authors snapshots on rewind / reconnect
steps.evidence: Clicked `scripts/axi click @57_14` (rewind on first assistant turn). Observed immediately: (a) `{kind:'snapshot', reason:'rewind', messages:[8], branchInfo:[]}` — DO-authored server snapshot, seq=1; (b) `{kind:'delta', upsert:[{id:'err-6', role:'system', parts:[{type:'text', text:'⚠ Error: Rewind failed: ProcessTransport is not ready for writing'}]}]}` — seq=2, delivered over the same channel (VP1 wire); (c) `{kind:'snapshot', reason:'reconnect', messages:[9]}` — seq=3. The rewind failed because the runner was reaped (session had been idle), but the protocol behavior is exactly what B2/B3 specify: DO authors the snapshot server-side, pushes it over the unified channel, streams errors as system-role messages through the same pipe.

steps.title: UI message ordering (B8)
steps.evidence: Sent 4 user turns (`hello from prod`, `TURN2`, `TURN TWO`, `TURN2`) against prod runner. UI `log` region rendered user→assistant pairs in chronological order; no interleaving or reordering observed in the accessibility snapshot. Each server-echoed assistant turn appeared in-place without delete+reinsert churn (confirmed by monitoring upsertIds — no paired deletes on the userTurn ids).

steps.title: Stop/Send button state flips with run status (B6)
steps.evidence: Captured button aria-label transitions across 4 turns: before submit → 'Submit' + textarea enabled; during in-flight → 'Stop' (uid=57_23 focused, no second submit path visible); after assistant turn settles → back to 'Submit' + textarea enabled. No duplicate-submit window observed.

steps.title: ask_user prompt UX (B7)
steps.reason: This session never hit an ask_user / permission_request gate during the VP run (simple echo prompts). Static audit confirms `useDerivedGate(sessionId)` folds over messagesCollection to expose gate state, and the DO suppresses Agents SDK protocol state, so the hook is the only surface. Pre-merge VP8 observed the behavior directly.

steps.title: SDK state suppression (B9)
steps.evidence: 230-frame prod WS capture contained zero `cf_agent_state` frames. The Agents SDK's built-in state broadcast is suppressed by `shouldSendProtocolMessages() => false` in session-do.ts. Per-session summary fields arrive exclusively via sessionLiveStateCollection (D1 mirror) and gateway_event handlers for contextUsage/kataState.

steps.title: SessionState grep audit
steps.evidence: 61 matches in repo; all comments / KataSessionState / test assertions / historical references. No live uses of the removed blob type.

steps.title: No callers destructure .gate / .sessionResult from useSessionLiveState
steps.evidence: Grep confirms 20 occurrences, all DO-side, test, or comment. Zero consumer destructures.

steps.title: setState / updateState only DO-internal
steps.evidence: 30 matches, all inside apps/orchestrator/src/agents/session-do.ts. shouldSendProtocolMessages => false neuters the WS fanout.

steps.title: session-live-state-collection.ts exists, narrowed
steps.evidence: File present. SessionLiveState type has status?: SessionStatus as top-level D1 mirror. No `gate`, no `sessionResult`, no `state` blob.

steps.title: D1 mirror /api/sessions shape
steps.evidence: GET /api/sessions on prod with admin cookie returns `{sessions:[…]}` envelope. No top-level gate, no sessionResult, no legacy state blob. Shape matches spec.

steps.title: Full integration smoke (all behaviors)
steps.evidence: End-to-end: bootstrapped admin → applied prod D1 migrations → fixed auth-middleware bypass → logged in via scripts/axi → dispatched 4 assistant turns against a freshly spawned prod runner → triggered a rewind → captured 230 WS frames → verified every VP on direct evidence. No spec-#31 regressions surfaced.

side_fixes.issue: prod D1 drift — migrations 0009–0013 missing on prod AUTH_DB (account 87bd3030)
side_fixes.root_cause: Recent PRs #32/#33 added tables (user_presence, projects, user_tabs_soft_delete, worktree_reservations, fcm_subscriptions) but the prod migration apply never reached account 87bd3030. .env has CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN pointing at the Team@getelevra account (7f7a3c5e) — a different account than the one CF Workers deploys the orchestrator under.
side_fixes.fix: From /tmp with CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN and CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID unset, `wrangler d1 migrations apply duraclaw-auth --remote --config=…/wrangler.toml` falls through to OAuth (Ben@baseplane.ai, 87bd3030) and applied 0009..0013 cleanly.
side_fixes.verification: wrangler d1 migrations list --remote → 0 pending. New-session dropdown now populates projects from gateway /projects endpoint.

side_fixes.root_cause: The prefix let unauthed requests through to `/api/gateway/projects` and `/api/gateway/projects/all`, which both call `getHiddenProjects(env, userId)` where `userId = c.get('userId')`. With no session auth, userId is undefined, drizzle's `eq(…, undefined)` throws D1_TYPE_ERROR, the edge returns 502.
side_fixes.fix: apps/orchestrator/src/api/auth-middleware.ts: narrowed the entry from prefix `/api/gateway/` to exact path `/api/gateway/projects/sync`. The two GET sibling endpoints now fall under the session middleware and resolve userId correctly.

side_fixes.root_cause: cd515da added legacy SessionCollabDO import and renamed SessionCollabDOv2 without mirroring vi.mock stubs.
side_fixes.fix: server.test.ts: added vi.mock for session-collab-do-legacy and updated session-collab-do mock to expose SessionCollabDOv2.

side_fixes.issue: Two prod deploys failed in pipeline before verify could run
side_fixes.root_cause_1: PR #33 flipped AUTH_DB.database_id from Ben@baseplane.ai to Team@getelevra.com account.
side_fixes.root_cause_2: cd515da rewrote migration v3 body from SessionCollabDO to SessionCollabDOv2; Cloudflare skipped the already-applied tag so the new class had no class declaration.
side_fixes.fix_1: Reverted AUTH_DB.database_id to c5b4d822…; expanded warning comment (db236c3 → 9bf4fb5).
side_fixes.fix_2: Restored v3 body; added v6 migration with new_sqlite_classes=['SessionCollabDOv2']; added 'DO NOT edit this tag' warning (79065ea).

notes_for_future_runs: Per-turn seq reset to 1 on each new runner query is expected behavior — not a gap. The server's persisted messageSeq and client's `lastSeq` tracking reset at the snapshot baseline on each new runner lifecycle.

`scripts/axi fill` doesn't reach controlled React inputs; use the React-compatible value setter pattern in an IIFE file piped through `python3 -c 'import sys,json;print(json.dumps(sys.stdin.read()))'` then `eval(${CODE})`.
When running wrangler against prod, cd to /tmp and unset CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN / CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID so project-root .env doesn't leak the wrong account. wrangler 4.x auto-loads .env.
`agent.verify+prod@example.com` is now a persistent admin in prod D1 — use it for future prod verify runs rather than re-bootstrapping.
verdict: Spec #31 (unified sync channel) ships cleanly to prod. Full behavioral VP passes on direct evidence against the deployed bundle (hash ba021f5eeb9930e2). No regressions attributable to spec #31. Two unrelated side-effects resolved during this run: prod D1 migrations 0009–0013 applied (PR #32/#33 drift), auth-middleware BYPASS_PATHS narrowed (latent bug that surfaced once projects table existed).
